description: The cognitive act of intentionally or unintentionally releasing a trace of a past experience from present awareness. It is framed as a natural and often healthy process of letting go.
rationale: A direct counterpart to 'theule' (remember), 'sheule' frames forgetting as a mindful release. 'she' uses the social/contemplative fricative to suggest a gentle letting go (Buddhist). The core vowel hiatus 'e-u' is reversed from 'theule', sonically representing moving from present awareness ('e') back into the deep, un-manifest ('u'). The final '-le' represents the flowing trace being released (Art Nouveau).
